disclosure: Generated training plates. They support scouting and sampling; they do not confirm a diagnosis.

assets.prompt: Use case: scientific-educational. Asset type: corrected photographic diagnostic atlas plate. Primary request: Correct the prior rice root aphid image so the single winged alate is the same realistic 1.5-2.5 mm scale as the wingless rice root aphids, only modestly larger, not a giant fly. Keep the root-zone colony composition and cannabis root plug context. Subject: Rhopalosiphum rufiabdominale; many waxy pale olive-to-reddish wingless nymphs/adults along fine cannabis roots; exactly one correctly scaled alate with aphid-shaped soft abdomen, short cornicles, antennae, six legs, and two pairs of transparent wings. Style/medium: photorealistic scientific documentary macro photograph, plausible anatomy and scale. Constraints: preserve exposed white cannabis roots and soilless plug; diagnostic sharpness; no other insect species; no illustration. Avoid: oversized alate, long fly-like legs, beetle or gnat anatomy, extra wings or legs, text, labels, arrows, logo, frame, watermark.

Use case: scientific-educational. Asset type: photographic diagnostic atlas plate for an indoor medicinal-cannabis IPM field guide. Primary request: Create a realistic documentary macro photograph of fungus gnats (Bradysia species) in diagnostic cannabis root-zone context. Scene/backdrop: damp dark soilless cannabis media beside the lower stem of a healthy indoor cannabis plant, with a thin patch of green algae at the media surface. Subject: one correctly scaled 2-4 mm adult fungus gnat standing just above the damp media, delicate dark mosquito-like body, long legs and antennae, clear narrow wings; at the exposed media edge include one slender translucent legless larva with a sharply visible black head capsule. Style/medium: photorealistic scientific documentary macro photography, natural greenhouse texture, accurate anatomy and scale. Composition/framing: low-angle close macro at media surface; adult and larva both visible and sharp enough for identification, cannabis lower stem and serrated lower leaf softly present for host context. Lighting/mood: neutral diffuse greenhouse light, accurate color. Constraints: adult must be a fungus gnat rather than a fruit fly, drain fly, or mosquito; larva must be translucent and wormlike with a black head; plausible scale. Avoid: text, labels, arrows, logos, frames, watermark, infographic layout, extra wings or legs, giant insects, fantasy colors, soil cross-section illustration.

Use case: scientific-educational. Asset type: photographic diagnostic atlas plate for an indoor medicinal-cannabis IPM field guide. Primary request: Create a realistic documentary macro photograph of western flower thrips (Frankliniella occidentalis) feeding on a young cannabis leaf. Scene/backdrop: tender serrated cannabis leaf and nearby growing tip inside a clean indoor grow; damaged leaf tissue remains unmistakably cannabis. Subject: two or three correctly scaled slender adult thrips with narrow fringed wings and tan-to-brown bodies, plus several smaller pale yellow wingless larvae. Show characteristic silvery scraped feeding scars and scattered tiny black fecal spots beside the feeding sites. Style/medium: photorealistic scientific documentary macro photography, natural surface texture, plausible anatomy and scale. Composition/framing: close oblique macro across the young leaf; adults, larvae, silver scarring, and black frass all readable without becoming a diagram. Lighting/mood: neutral diffuse greenhouse light, accurate color and detail. Constraints: thrips must be slender, about 1-2 mm relative to leaf trichomes and veins; adult wings visibly fringed; six legs; no other pests. Avoid: text, labels, arrows, logos, frames, watermark, infographic layout, giant insects, beetle bodies, butterfly-like wings, extra limbs, fantasy colors.

Use case: scientific-educational. Asset type: photographic diagnostic atlas plate for an indoor medicinal-cannabis IPM field guide. Primary request: Create a realistic documentary macro photograph of a two-spotted spider mite infestation (Tetranychus urticae) on the underside of a cannabis leaf. Scene/backdrop: underside of a mature serrated cannabis fan leaf, veins and cystolith hairs clearly recognizable, indoor grow softly out of focus. Subject: several correctly scaled oval yellow-green adult spider mites, each with two obvious dark lateral body spots and eight legs; include smaller juveniles and clusters of tiny translucent spherical eggs near veins; sparse fine silk webbing stretched between vein junctions. Style/medium: photorealistic scientific documentary macro photography, natural texture, plausible microscopic scale and anatomy. Composition/framing: extreme macro along the lower leaf surface with mites, eggs, and webbing all diagnostically sharp while leaf context remains clear. Lighting/mood: neutral diffuse greenhouse light, accurate color. Constraints: adults must be mites with eight legs, not insects; two dark spots clearly visible; eggs round and translucent; fine webbing, not a heavy spider web. Avoid: text, labels, arrows, logos, frames, watermark, infographic layout, giant mites, six-legged adults, extra limbs, fantasy colors, dew drops mistaken for eggs.

Use case: scientific-educational. Asset type: photographic diagnostic atlas plate for an indoor medicinal-cannabis IPM field guide. Primary request: Create a realistic microscope-style macro photograph of broad mites (Polyphagotarsonemus latus) and their diagnostic eggs on a distorted cannabis meristem. Scene/backdrop: tender cannabis shoot tip with small blistered, hardened, downward-curled new leaves; visible fine plant hairs and meristem texture under high magnification. Subject: several extremely small translucent pale-yellow oval adult broad mites, eight short legs concentrated near the front half of the body, under 0.2 mm scale; beside them show multiple clear oval eggs with the species-characteristic rows of raised white circular ridges or dots over the egg surface. Style/medium: photorealistic scientific photomicrograph, high-magnification documentary imaging, plausible anatomy and scale. Composition/framing: close microscope field including both mites and ridged eggs on the damaged young cannabis tissue; the curled meristem morphology remains recognizable. Lighting/mood: neutral transmitted-and-diffuse microscope illumination, accurate pale colors, high detail. Constraints: mites and eggs must be much smaller than leaf hairs and veins; distinctive ornamented eggs are essential; no webbing. Avoid: text, labels, arrows, logos, frames, watermark, infographic layout, giant mites, spider-like long legs, smooth spherical eggs, extra limbs, fantasy colors.

Use case: scientific-educational. Asset type: photographic diagnostic atlas plate for an indoor medicinal-cannabis IPM field guide. Primary request: Create a realistic high-magnification documentary photograph of hemp russet mites (Aculops cannabicola) feeding on a bronzed cannabis petiole and adjacent stem epidermis. Subject: many microscopic pale cream-to-yellow eriophyid mites, elongated carrot- or worm-shaped bodies tapering at the rear, each with only two pairs of tiny legs clustered at the head end; a few tiny translucent oval eggs nearby. Lighting/mood: neutral diffuse microscope lighting, accurate color. Constraints: mites must be under 0.2 mm, much smaller than plant hairs, wormlike rather than oval, exactly two pairs of legs at the front, no webbing. Avoid: text, labels, arrows, logos, frames, watermark, infographic layout, giant mites, spider-shaped mites, eight obvious legs, fantasy colors, smooth undamaged host tissue.

Use case: scientific-educational. Asset type: photographic diagnostic atlas plate for an indoor medicinal-cannabis IPM field guide. Use case: scientific-educational
Asset type: integrated pest management field-guide symptom-comparison photograph
Primary request: Create a highly realistic documentary photograph comparing a healthy medicinal cannabis plant with a plant showing a syndrome compatible with hop latent viroid (HLVd), without implying that appearance alone confirms infection.
Scene/backdrop: The same indoor controlled-environment grow room, identical pots, substrate, cultivar, age, training, light, and camera distance.
Subject: Two same-cultivar Cannabis sativa plants side by side. The healthy plant is vigorous with normal internode spacing, dense branching, broad healthy fan leaves and full well-developed flowers. The symptomatic comparison plant is visibly reduced in vigour with shortened internodes, smaller leaves, weaker lateral growth, and smaller sparse underdeveloped flowers. Both plants remain alive; symptoms are subtle but unmistakable as a comparative syndrome.
Style/medium: True-to-life agricultural documentary photography, realistic plant architecture and flower development, no illustration or infographic look.
Composition/framing: Straight-on full-plant comparison, both root zones and canopies in the same frame and scale, no divider line and no text.
Lighting/mood: Even neutral horticultural lighting, accurate green tones, sharp diagnostic detail.
Constraints: Same cultivar and environmental treatment implied by matching setup; cannabis-specific morphology; no text, labels, arrows, logos, borders, frames, captions, test results, pathogen particles, or watermark.
Avoid: cartoon before-and-after, dramatic death, severe nutrient burn, mites or visible pests, mosaic patterns, different pot sizes, different growth stages, oversized dense flowers on symptomatic plant, claiming visual confirmation.
